title: The Echoing Attic
idea: A young woman inherits her estranged grandmother's dusty, antique house. While exploring the attic, she finds an old music box. When she winds it up, it plays a faint, unsettling melody. Suddenly, she hears a child's whisper from the corner. She dismisses it as her imagination, but then the music box stops abruptly, and the whisper repeats, louder this time, and clearly says her name. She looks around, terrified, but sees nothing. She cautiously winds the music box again, and this time, as the melody plays, she sees a fleeting shadow dart across the far wall, mirroring the movements of a child playing. The whispering grows more insistent with each rotation of the music box, revealing fragmented memories and pleas for help that seem to belong to a lost child trapped within the house's history. The final shot is her winding the music box for the third time, the whisper now a chilling scream, and the music box lid slams shut on its own, plunging the attic into darkness with just her terrified gasp echoing.
targetAudience: Fans of supernatural horror, ghost stories, and suspenseful mysteries.
